3139387EFDD4652622C29413791795F8: - Configures the input as a breakglass and once set you will need to link this input to a door.  Emergency breakglass is often wired directly into the door's power circuit, allowing the door to be unlocked in case of emergency. This however bypasses the lock output state so even if the controller says the door is locked, the door can actually be opened. By wiring the breakglass detector into the system and linking it to a door, the door will be marked as unlocked and identify the cause as the breakglass. A warning will also be created, prompting that the breakglass should be fixed and that the door is not secure.

3FC8DDB78F95E3A7275136AA3749B048: .  A floor in free access will cause all of the lift cars that can access that floor to enable the button for that floor, allowing any user to select that floor without needing to present a card. A secured floor requires a card to be presented in a lift car before a button linked to the floor would be enabled. A locked out floor will prevent a button linked to the floor from enabling, even if a user with permission to the floor presents their card.
409819E124DE76883396E87A22577CF8: This trigger condition will be true when the selected Hardware Module has detected tampering on a door reader, remaining true while the issue persists. This condition can apply to the Controller or any door expansion modules.

42CCD64A0A8A3ED594509DBFC0803038: The first step is to create Lift Floors. Lift Floors represent a destination that a button in a lift car would take the user to, and multiple lift cars may have a button that can take the user to the same lift floor. In some cases there may be multiple lift floors created for the same physical floor in a building, for example if the lift car has two doors and there are different buttons for 7A and 7B. In this case, different floors can be created with different permissions for each side of the car. Alternatively, if a particular lift car needs its access to be restricted to only certain users, for example a service elevator, there could be normal floors created for the other cars, along with another set of service floors for the service Lift Car, with only maintenance workers having permission to those floors.
436FC7AE8A8EBAB75D9615D3E6DEC7A5: Editing and saving of a User within the system resets the invalid credential count for all readers and clears reader lockout if it has been triggered. This allows cards to be assigned to users by swiping an unknown card at a reader, then assigning that unknown card swipe to a user, without worrying about triggering reader lockout.

Inception hardware modules are devices that are added to the Inception Controller to provide additional inputs, outputs, doors and readers. These hardware modules require an RS-485 LAN connection to the controller to function. If this connection is severed, the module does not function and is considered to be offline and all detector inputs on that module are considered tampered with.

The device needs to be on the same local network as the Inception Controller or be connected to the Inception Controllers Wi-Fi Hotspot (requires the optional Inception Wireless Adapter). A user can also remotely connect to the web interface using the Skytunnel service, Skytunnel is a hassle free internet based connection method.

6A1B44037F0BE1D7140220B17E0875BB: The site code is a unique facility code that is contained within the data on card type credentials, the site code acts as a second authentication for the credential number. Cards for a site are generally purchased with a single site code, however, multiple purchases of cards can lead to one site using multiple site codes. The card format determines how the data from the card that is badged at a reader is interpreted. This will determine what part of the card data is the credential number and what part of the data is the site code.
6AA1D5127F22A1D7F3479CFB842E2569: Create or select an existing user and assign the credentials required for the user to interact with the system. Credentials are what identify a user and must be unique between all users. Credentials include the PIN number used to request at access keypad readers or log in to the LCD terminal, cards or biometric data for access control and RF remotes for remote control. Also included are the credentials required to log on to the web interface, both via the local network and over the internet via SkyTunnel (if enabled). Web access requires a username, password and a profile assigned, which determines which pages the user can view and how they can interact with those pages.
6ADE2DA821F49F38D62D2F5ABCB7B941: Counters are primarily intended for usage in combination with automated actions, to allow advanced automation functionality to be configured, such as triggering an action once a certain number of events have occurred, e.g. an input activation counter or reader badge counter. Counter state values are persisted across system power cycles.
6C5A7315F40B883636B57F4D4A6136B8: - This uses the Inception Controllers network connection with internet access to communicate with the SkyTunnel server. SkyTunnel is a cloud-based service that links two outbound connections, which means that you will not require port forwarding. Once the event is received by the SkyTunnel server it is delivered to a monitoring station. This works out of the box and requires no other specialized hardware.

73B16CD25029C5074D1AFCF47F155D40: Essentially, low-level lift integration involves wiring of outputs in the Inception system to lift buttons in a lift car, allowing the individual buttons to be enabled or disabled by the Inception system. Then, a user is able to present their card at a reader located in the lift car and based on their permissions, certain floor selection buttons can be enabled. In addition, the lift buttons can also be wired into Inputs in the Inception system, allowing Inception to know which button a user pressed.
74BEAFEBA77621D34D627D5F0AFC83FA: Fields consisting of lists of items (e.g. Credentials, Remote Fob, Permissions) can only accept a single item at a time due to the limitations of the CSV format. For newly imported Users, the single item will become the only item in the list field. For existing users, the single item will be appended to the existing list of items (if it is not already present). In the case where you wish to import a User(s) with 2 credentials assigned to them, for example: you can CSV import the User(s) initially with a single credential number specified in the Credentials field, and then perform another CSV import with the same Users' names but with the 2nd credential number in the Credentials field, and the existing User will gain the 2nd credential number in their Credentials list.

908E3ACFF0D094AEA5DDA3D2C527B4B1: The SLAM is capable of holding 2000 cards in an internal cache, tracking whether access was granted the last time it was presented at a reader and remembering this per-door controlled by the SLAM. 91BBDC9953ED2CCC9A7841D84DF16C81: - Each input hardware point on the controller can have its functionality configured. Although traditionally these have only been able to be used for the purpose labelled on the module, Inception gives you the flexibility to define the purpose per input.  EOL resistors are required for all input behaviours except REX/REN and Generic Switch/Button.
936906CD74BE03D73EEA16853F8FCC2F: A storage block should represent a logical set of storage units with clear entry and exit points controlled with access control. For simple sites, there may be one block for the entire site with entry and exit gate readers used for automatic unit control in the block. Blocks could also represent a building full of units, and the entry and exit readers for that building would be used by the block.

958A9DA229B7C48BA446C4FC05F8179F: User expiry is commonly used for contractors or other users who require access to the system for a limited period of time. It allows their user to be configured in advance with credentials and permissions, but only activate at a future time. Similarly, the user can be automatically restricted to prevent their access at a date and time in the future, removing the need for a user to remember to manually edit or delete the user in the future.

Are you sure you want to reset this controller's IO mode? This allows you to switch the controller between using simple inputs and outputs and being able to configure readers and doors onboard. 

 This will not affect any other items in the system or any inputs and outputs linked to other hardware.

Note, any previously configured inputs, outputs, doors and readers connected directly to the controller itself will need to be reprogrammed.

996D59615BD5AD999B192DCDDA3CFD8F: When configuring a module, you will be stepped through multiple sections of programming. 9A14A80E698E84B9527A8A3C620B26BA: This trigger condition will pulse whenever any user performs the selected Reader Action at the selected reader.
9C5E305C38BA198293BC720C785BFCDA: The reader is the access point that a user interacts with to unlock a door. Depending on the door there may be a reader on one side or on both sides of the door. The reader is usually configured and associated with a door in the configuration hardware menu. This page provides additional reader specific options to configure the operation of the reader.
9CE83106C3B7E6DAD3F394EC8CCE3485: Alternatively, to better manage common settings, a whole site block could exist with entry and exit readers to control all of the units, set common resecure and unlocked too long times, and so on. Additional blocks can then be created just for local building automation. Those additional blocks wouldn't have readers configured, and would just contain the units in a section of the building and the outputs to control lighting in that section. If using this approach, be sure to set the Unit Unlocked Too Long and Unit Auto-Resecure times to None to not override the values in the main Block.
9D6DDD0A1A8F9B5C71A20F345FDA8742: This page will dynamically display the state of all the attached peripheral devices. Inception peripherals are devices that users interact with such as SIFER Readers that attach to hardware modules. These peripherals are monitored, for example, SIFER or OSDP readers are monitored to determine if they are still connected, unlike a Wiegand reader. This page allows an installer to identify and troubleshoot issues with peripheral devices.
9DDB2090B43900902EEC7126009E6A0E: By default, SIFER readers will be able to read SIFER credentials and ECOLITE credentials. The Multi-Format version of the SIFER reader will also be capable of reading the CSN data of various third party 13.56Mhz credentials.

Report will show the amount of time between each badge in and each badge out, listing per-user each site visit over the report timeframe. Read-In and Read-Out readers must be specified.

A437A57A2187F6C1FD0C01130137040C: Cards are automatically removed from this cache for several reasons; if access is denied to a door when the card is used, after 1 month of inactivity, or if the 2000 limit is reached then as new cards are added to the cache the oldest cards are removed. This attempts to allow an intelligent offline behaviour where the people who use a door recently will still be able to access the door in the event that the door module loses connectivity with the Inception controller.
A4639B9360D9674C0A79DFCB3131C37D: Inception's web interface can be configured to allow access via HTTPS for a greater level of security (compared to standard HTTP) when configuring the system. HTTPS access can be enabled either in combination with HTTP access, or with HTTP disabled for the highest level of security and encryption. When HTTP access is disabled, plain HTTP requests made to the Inception controller will be redirected to use the HTTPS protocol instead.

B3406A1AECF29825C32C903741BF923A: Credentials are what identify a user and must be unique between all users. Credentials include the PIN number used to access keypad readers or log into an LCD terminal, cards for access control and RF remotes for remote control. You can also provide the user a username and password to grant them access to the Inception Web Interface; the user will also require a Web Page Profile, which determines which pages they can view and how they can interact with those pages.

C49B0C607E644E90E0A5CEEE238C684A: - SIFER/OSDP or Wiegand readers are installed on one side or both sides of a door. The readers provide the controller with card or PIN information to identify a user who is requesting access. If they have permission to access the door, the door can unlock.
C50C2F8E7CF5F08F3DEFFCBB853D145F: This trigger condition will pulse whenever the selected User performs the selected Reader Action at the chosen reader.
C520492F8B87AB63EA26E75791342D85: The default time period is called Working Hours; this time period is commonly used to trigger events to occur during a typical working day, such as internal doors unlocking at the start of the work day or turning on an output that controls lighting. CA8248298736550916081D3EC7C74527: - Generates a report which shows a list of Users have been on site each day and for how long, based on their first and last reader badge events for the day (a single day is treated as being from 12am-11:59pm). Optionally filterable by Users and Doors. This type of report is recommended if Users are only on site during regular daytime hours (i.e. no night shifts going past midnight), and if your site don't have strictly defined in/out readers.
CA83D7BD90A059DF2706625277558813: This page is used to identify where a user is located, this may be required in an emergency situation or simply when you want to easily find a person. CF4C471D0EFD9F65B109A7EE98D05A11: The only method for connection to the web interface is via a wired or wireless network. The default network settings are configured for wired Ethernet using DHCP, this means that if the Inception Controller is connected to an existing network it will automatically be assigned an IP address. This page allows you to configure the additional setting for the Ethernet connection and even enable Wi-Fi or allow access to the web interface via Skytunnel.

D2F9C098D38706BB385B8A79B307B31F: Users are granted permission to access lift floors, so that when they present a card or PIN at a reader within a lift car, the buttons that would take them to the floors they can access would be enabled by Inception. Lift floors can also be in one of three states,
D39127ADFDD947068DD58DA241B89C2A: . A floor in free access will cause the lift cars that can access that floor to enable the button for that floor, allowing any user to select that floor without needing to present a card. A secured floor requires a card to be presented in a lift car before a button linked to the floor would be enabled. A locked out floor will prevent a button linked to the floor from enabling, even if a user with permission to the floor presents their card.
D466944DF2E8AC9078596A6BCC458D12: Three failed credential attempts in a row trigger a lockout on this reader which will last for 90 seconds. Once triggered, any credentials presented to the reader will be ignored until the lockout time expires. After the lockout time has expired, repeat invalid credentials will cause the 90 second lockout time to restart.
D646907CED03170E14905BDA7456C2F5: This dialog allows you to assign a credential to a user based off of a recent (i.e. within the last 24 hours) unassigned card read event.
D6FCA9A0D8C39083B0D1120AAFF1EAF8: - The hardware point refers to the physical connectors on the Standard LAN Access Module. These connectors are labelled on the module, therefore each hardware point in the list has a matching connector on the SLAM.
D7C506998E69EC0FEBCBFADFADB8204C: - If the behaviour of the output is set to an access control function such as DOTL, valid, invalid or beeper, then the linked door option must be set. The linked door option is used to determine which door the outputs are associated with and controlled by. The linked door option is not available if the output behaviour is set to generic output as this does not relate to access control.
D9FCDC4A3F477055B9385FCB56BF32BA: Note: This option should only be enabled if the LCD terminal is used solely for access control or there is potentially a security issue due to the LCD terminal being located in a public place.
DA3C864E0A7B1B4921DC2205BD944CED: Credential templates contain information such as how the credentials presented at readers should be interpreted (e.g. as cards, fingerprints, facial scan data) and the card format being used (if applicable). Credentials are then assigned to users by specifying a credential template and the credential number. Credential templates can be configured with a specific card format and include a site code to match cards, or can be configured to use raw card information (direct entry), which uses the full card data as the credential number.
DA8D3D361F97AAA19BA08031A8E33DBF: The override options are used to temporarily change the normal behaviour of a door, locking a door that should be unlocked or unlocking a door that is normally locked. A door can also be placed in the
DA99DC749DEE1EADDFB79503776A3E49: This page is used to configure the Inner Range Mobile Access connection settings, connecting to a corresponding Mobile Access system using a pairing code generated from the Mobile Access portal.

DC197223D78BCA5246443B7B26D85804: Wiegand keypad readers allow the entering of a user's PIN number in order to request access through a door. E11BD80DC45538BD569FD570F78336A4: field is used by the Inception system to distinguish between different categories of credential data when it is received from a card reader, a fingerprint/iris scanner, or another source. The type of the credential template is taken into account when users attempt to access a door with Dual or Triple Credential Authentication enabled, for example, Dual Credential Authentication requires that at least 2 unique types of credentials are presented at a door reader (e.g. Card type and Fingerprint type) before access is granted.
E35E62146B255A53F2000C5FF05D71A8: Allows the user to select when this door's override state is automatically cleared (this does not occur if the override is Lockout).
E3AAF08C0D70547E84EC53D63BB32E58: You can enable Skytunnel if a remote connection from a device outside of the local network to the Inception Controller is required. E7076002620C730B68D6938D001932D3: A credential is a uniquely numbered card, fob, or other type of identifiable data (e.g. a biometric fingerprint or face scan). The credential number is read by a reader at a door to identify which user is attempting to access the door.
E73A6A2B86568C48266B2001796765EE: Enable this option to require that a PIN number must be provided at some point to access the Emergency request options.
E8139E458C98BC39DD7E8FEF58198878: During the day, a user could use their Card or PIN to access a lift car for convenience, however after hours, the site could require that both Card and PIN are used for higher security. Similarly, access modes could differ based on weekday vs weekends and so on.
E9576A56AE27486F1B1142B7BE4B0259: The reader lockout feature helps to protect the system from brute force attacks where multiple access cards or PIN numbers are attempted in order to impersonate a user. If multiple access cards or PIN credentials are presented at a reader that do not match a user, reader lockout is triggered. Only credentials that do not match a user are counted, attempts that are denied due to permissions do not count.
EA1B9B4D664884165737FAD11AEA20C3: A lift car represents a physical lift car or button panel that the user interacts with. Lift cars require hardware outputs in the Inception system to be wired to the lift button panel so they can enable and disable whether the lift button works. Inputs can also be wired into the button to indicate when the button has been pressed. Using this information and ability in addition to a credential reader, allows Inception to control which floor buttons a user is allowed to press when they present their card, biometric, or PIN credential.
EB27E6C4C83073BF48C53C56BFAFFB0B: If there are a certain number of login attempts allowed for each user, they will be locked out after exceeding those login attempts and it will be displayed on this page. Quick control will allow you to end the lockout early for this user. This page can also forgive all users with a login lockout at the same time as long as your priority ranking is higher than each user being forgiven.

EDC4810706AD04E7FDD8CFA2AC8752D6: When using CSV Import to update users, if no value is specified for a field then no change is made. This means that, for example; to update an email address for a user, no permission group, PIN, credential, or notes need to be specified.
EE827E2B9EE19D1FB170BD0E9C48A555: when the terminal is logged off. This option is useful in situations where terminals may already be in proximity to a door, and an exit button / enter button is required. This feature avoids the need to install a separate access button.
F1E847D2242E5ED5514C25F7F4EEBEAD: The duration of time that the lift buttons will be enabled for when a user presents a valid card or PIN to a configured car reader. This duration should be set to allow the user enough time to make their floor selection after presenting their credential.
F2E195CAF572E47313CBF97BBAAE6E8A: WARNING: This action will reset the entire Inception system and render the system non-operational for up to 60 seconds while it restarts. It is highly recommended that you make sure you are on-site when resetting the system, in case problems occur and remote access becomes unavailable. Are you sure you want to continue?
F3E9AEC8B2A376732665019819113DD0: This page is used to view the state of peripheral devices. Peripherals are the advanced devices that can be connected to hardware modules, like Unibus expanders on 8 Input Expanders, or SIFER or OSDP readers on SLAMs or the Controller.
F59D69F40B4E06CCA8C2A6E2897DFF43: [Configuration > Access Control > Mobile Access > Mobile Access Connection Settings]
F64601BC81C39A06E686DD064063BB30: Allows users to be notified if a user has triggered an anti-passback violation at a door. The doors that may be notified on are determined by the
F6ADB2598BF449080AA575AB9B62D8B7: Allows users to be notified if an LCD Terminal triggers its PIN Lockout mode. This could indicate that someone is attempting to guess a user's PIN to gain access to the system.
F70D6DB7528E39148DC8DE4AB352AF0A: [Configuration > Access Control > Storage Unit > Storage Units]
F893D003B8D675017CF6775CB91240A1: Allows a user to unlock the door with a 2-badge or 3-badge action at an associated reader, and clear the override with a single or 2-badge action. The user must have override permission for the door in order to unlock or clear its override state.
F901B4462BF117C9D761E3C096E827B3: With this option enabled, the readers will be limited to reading SIFER credentials only. There may be a slight performance improvement with this option as the reader only needs to read one format instead of testing multiple formats. It also offers slightly higher reader security as only the high security SIFER credentials are accepted.
FB592F628A5C6CCB5A7FAD6B1105A2A3: Web interface lockout is triggered when multiple failed web login attempts occur in a row. The number of attempts required to trigger web interface lockout is three, preventing any users from logging in for a short period of time. If the failed login attempts continue, the amount of time that the web interface is locked out for will increase, reducing the effectiveness of a brute-force attack.
FC88454C108F4715F4FDECE64FAC90F2: This trigger condition will be true while the lift floor has been locked out and will only turn false when this state has been overridden.
FE8BA9F3364BA7ADBC52F04CAD940BBE: When assigning a credential to a user, the first step is to select the credential template. This instructs the system how to interpret that data and what site code to expect. Once a credential template has been added to the user, the credential number is typed in.
FE93BB7C2EEF2948A437FA29B5F6113F: Anti-passback is commonly used on sites whose policies rely on accurate user locations. This could be for time on site reports, accurate muster reports for OH&S compliance and safety, or secure locations in the building. It can also help protect resources such as a carpark, preventing access into the carpark, a card being passed back and another driver entering with the same credential.
FEBF590786BE1445E1658AC2AB8911EB: Specifies the readers that can control this lift car that allows a user to present either their PIN, card, or other credential. Multiple readers can be assigned to the same lift car if required, allowing readers to be mounted in different locations or be of different types.
FFA281C5DF2065FE971BFE5CFF990F1F: Credential templates are used when assigning credentials (e.g. cards, biometric data) to users. The credential template contains information such as the credential type, the site code and the card format of the cards, if applicable.

FFD026296BDE2DDEA6E35471F0E425B2: - The hardware point refers to the physical connectors on the Intelligent LAN Access Module. These connectors are labelled on the module, therefore each hardware point in the list has a matching connector on the ILAM.
048863D0362AAF81FDBCCD37A4ED9B82: When this page is first opened, all the current states for the outputs are displayed and the page will dynamically update as the outputs change state. The state of the output can easily be identified by the colour of the output and the text description in the
05150FDA4F2CFB3F7B740288F59B8C7D: control. This option allows you to specify a network share location, a domain name, and the domain user credentials to use when copying the output file to the network share location.
0EDAE5DE44BC810D6680139895055305: If enabled, the physical hardware output would be in the opposite state to the one shown in the system.
11F25830E2351FEF1FFC3EF6F1965754: This page is used to easily set outputs as user controllable outputs so that they can be controlled directly by users, either via the Inception web interface or LCD Terminals.
16879FF05AC3240B1D52ECA95CCB7A2F: - Each output hardware point on the controller can have its function configured. Although these have been traditionally only been able to be used for the purpose labelled on the module, Inception gives you the flexibility to define the purpose of each output.
195C529950352CFA6126535738EC6095: This trigger condition will be true while all storage units in the chosen storage block are currently secure.
1B4A55975A3396CEEA0F3253740B6256: page, as well as their ability to manually control integrated devices on that page. Control of integrated devices allows a user to send manual commands to the integrated device.
1FA153582D84E9DD65CBACF6F30B458B: To test a module, select it from the module list, the page will update to display the current state of all the configured inputs and outputs for the module. If there are inputs or outputs missing from this list they have not been configured in the
2018629441A5EDBCE079506E77848329: This page is used to create and edit automated actions to allow additional automation functionality on the Inception system.
20346FBFD31F14540332F3FFA0B23773: This page is used to view and control the state of counters. You can increment, decrement and manually set counter values from this page. To control a counter, you must have the appropriate user permissions and web page profile.
204D11389B6DBD81A4A9A331AF802265: This trigger condition will be true as long as the chosen input has been isolated, and will only turn false when de-isolated.
227D985C16E21CCA22442D95F7A01554: This page is used by the installer during the initial installation and ongoing maintenance of the Inception system. Due to the fact that time periods are heavily used in user permissions and automation it is recommended that permissions for this page be limited to the installer or the system administrator.
281A94F372F78B7CAE8A922D0DB6F67D: - The hardware point refers to the physical connectors on the 8-Aux Expander. These connectors are labelled on the module, therefore each hardware point in the list has a matching connector on the 8-Aux Expander.
2B2932B1AE0CBB9B635BF7C370093235: This trigger condition will be true as long as the chosen input is secure.
2CAE5F41DCEEB47CF2286A99A5A02951: Allows lift buttons to be configured for the lift car. Lift buttons represent the physical button within a lift car which when pressed will take the user to a floor. The configuration of a button that this lift car can control requires both a
30924764C6A981C0E38BBA40E4227ABB: Specifies how the scheduled task should send out the resulting output file after it is produced.
319C5CF568F25F1D64773DB80C86A679: The automated action is configured in two parts; the trigger condition and the action responses. The trigger condition determines when this automated action is true or false. When the trigger condition changes from false to true, a set of actions can be automatically executed. Similarly, when the trigger condition changes from true to false, a different set of actions can be automatically executed.
31FE1C316D5D57EFB421227A1C5E8374: This trigger condition will be true while the current system date and time matches an active time block in the selected time period.
353C2893E28BD0EFDE5B5D2BA7316B6D: This trigger condition will be true while there are one or more storage units in the chosen storage block that are pending secure due to unsealed inputs.
